subject: Chemistry
chapter: Organic Chemistry: Some Basic Principles and Techniques

definitions.example: For example, the 2p orbital is a dumbbell-shaped atomic orbital with one nodal plane.
definitions.definition: Atomic orbitals are three-dimensional regions around the nucleus of an atom where the probability of finding an electron is maximum. These are spherical in shape and are described by three quantum numbers: n, l, and m.

definitions.example: For example, the sp3 hybrid orbital is formed by mixing one s and three p orbitals.
definitions.definition: Hybrid orbitals are formed by the mixing of two or more atomic orbitals to create a new set of orbitals that are suitable for bonding. This results in the formation of a new energy level.

definitions.example: For example, the C-H bond in methane is a sigma bond.
definitions.definition: A sigma bond is a type of covalent bond that results from the head-on overlap of orbitals. It is symmetrical about the bond axis and is strongest along the bond axis.

definitions.example: For example, the C=C bond in ethene is a pi bond.
definitions.definition: A pi bond is a type of covalent bond that results from the side-by-side overlap of p orbitals. It is unsymmetrical about the bond axis and is weakest along the bond axis.

definitions.example: For example, the bond angle in water (H2O) is 104.5° due to the presence of two lone pairs on the oxygen atom.
definitions.definition: Bond angle is the angle between two bonds in a molecule. It is usually determined by the geometry of the molecule and the presence of lone pairs.

definitions.example: For example, fluorine has the highest electronegativity value among all elements.
definitions.definition: Electronegativity is the ability of an atom to attract electrons towards itself in a covalent bond. It increases with the increase in atomic number.

definitions.example: For example, the molecular orbital diagram of ethene (C2H4) shows the formation of a pi bond.
definitions.definition: Molecular orbital theory is a method of describing the electronic structure of molecules. It considers the overlap of atomic orbitals to form molecular orbitals.

definitions.example: For example, cis-2-butene and trans-2-butene are stereoisomers.
definitions.definition: Stereoisomerism is the phenomenon of having the same molecular formula and bond sequence but different three-dimensional arrangements of atoms.

definitions.example: For example, L-glutamic acid and D-glutamic acid are enantiomers.
definitions.definition: Enantiomers are non-superimposable mirror images of each other. They have the same physical and chemical properties but differ in optical activity.

definitions.example: For example, the carbon atom in L-glutamic acid is a chiral centre.
definitions.definition: A chiral centre is an atom that is bonded to four different groups. It is responsible for the formation of enantiomers.

definitions.example: For example, butane and isobutane are isomers.
definitions.definition: Isomerism is the phenomenon of having the same molecular formula but different molecular structures.

definitions.example: For example, the -OH group in alcohols determines their acidic nature.
definitions.definition: A functional group is a specific group of atoms within a molecule that determines its chemical properties.

definitions.example: For example, chloromethane (CH3Cl) is an alkyl halide.
definitions.definition: An alkyl halide is an organic compound that contains a halogen atom bonded to an alkyl group.

definitions.example: For example, sodium hydroxide is a reagent used in the saponification of fats and oils.
definitions.definition: A reagent is a substance that is used to react with another substance to produce a chemical change.

definitions.example: For example, water is a solvent used in the extraction of caffeine from tea leaves.
definitions.definition: A solvent is a substance that dissolves other substances to form a solution.

definitions.example: For example, the distillation of crude oil separates different fractions of petroleum products.
definitions.definition: Distillation is a method of separating a mixture of liquids based on differences in their boiling points.

definitions.example: For example, paper chromatography is used to separate the components of a mixture based on differences in their mobility.
definitions.definition: Chromatography is a method of separating a mixture of substances based on differences in their physical and chemical properties.

definitions.example: For example, infrared spectroscopy is used to determine the functional groups present in an organic compound.
definitions.definition: Spectroscopy is a method of analyzing the interaction between matter and electromagnetic radiation.

definitions.example: For example, NMR spectroscopy is used to determine the structure of organic compounds.
definitions.definition: NMR spectroscopy is a method of analyzing the nucleus of an atom in a magnetic field.

definitions.example: For example, mass spectrometry is used to determine the molecular weight of an organic compound.
definitions.definition: Mass spectrometry is a method of analyzing the mass-to-charge ratio of ions.
